response.score_text:
Uh-oh. Out of 100 possible points, there's definitely room to improve this page. 

Ecograder prioritizes a holistic approach to digital sustainability reporting. Each report includes quantifiable metrics to help you reduce emissions and improve performance. We also share best-in-class web design practices that aren't as easy to quantify from an ecological perspective. 

Read on to learn specific action items you can take to improve this page and your website overall.

categories.category_content: By compressing image file size and removing unused items from a page you'll not only reduce emissions but faster-downloading pages make users happier as well. Below are some things you can do to reduce the size of this page.

metrics.name: Reduce Overall Page Weight
metrics.impact_text:
Webpages that use unnecessary scripts, media, or other elements can slow down page performance, frustrate users, and increase both download times and emissions. 

Make sure you serve fast, lightweight pages to users by removing any unnecessary elements. 

Learn more about how to do this in the recommendations below.

Unused Javascript0 / 100

There is unused JavaScript associated with this page. According to Ecograder's calculations, 5 scripts could potentially be removed. This might shave off 323.68 kb from your page, speeding up download time by 120 ms.

Unused CSS Rules100 / 100

Well done! There are no unused CSS Rules on this page.

Total Byte Weight0 / 100

Whoa! This page is 9161 kb. It scored 0 out of 100 for page weight. You can significantly improve performance by optimizing 10 items on this page.
metrics.resources_text: Learn how page weight budgets can help you reach sustainability goals

metrics.name: Remove Unused Code
metrics.impact_text: Plugins, widgets, and third-party frameworks often introduce unnecessary code snippets into your page which can adversely impact download speed and performance.

Unminified CSS100 / 100

Well done! All the CSS associated with this page has been optimized. Minifying scripts removes extra characters, improving page download speeds and reducing both data usage and emissions.

Unused CSS Rules100 / 100

We did not detect any unused CSS Rules on this page.

Unminified Javascript100 / 100

Well done! All JavaScript associated with this page has been minified.

Minifying scripts removes extra characters, improving page download speeds and reducing both data usage and emissions.

Duplicated Javascript Insight100 / 100

We did not detect any duplicated JavaScript on this page.

Unused Javascript0 / 100

There is unused JavaScript associated with this page. According to Ecograder's calculations, 5 scripts could potentially be removed. This might shave off 323.68 kb from your page, speeding up download time by 120 ms.

Legacy Javascript Insight50 / 100

There is outdated JavaScript associated with this page. Legacy scripts can slow down pages and cause potential security risks. You should fix this.

Document Latency Insight100 / 100

It appears as though this page uses compression to deliver text-based resources. Good work.
metrics.resources_text: Read more about how to find and remove unused scripts and code

categories.category_content: Creating an accessible user experience that is free from barriers or intrusions helps people find what they need and accomplish tasks quickly. This is important because end consumer device use can potentially account for up to 52% of a digital product or service's overall emissions. Here are a few recommendations for providing your users with a better experience.

metrics.name: Improve Page Rendering
metrics.impact_text: Studies have shown that users leave pages that don't display content quickly. "Above-the-fold" page content should render on a user's device in 2.5 seconds or less. This is especially crucial in low-bandwidth areas and for users with older devices.

metrics.action_text:
Render Blocking Insight0 / 100

You scored 0 out of 100 for using scripts or stylesheets that slow down page render time. There is room to improve this by optimizing the URLs listed below.

Largest Contentful Paint49 / 100

This page scored 49 out of 100 based on how quickly this page displays content. This is okay, but there's still room to improve this score by identifying elements that impede page rendering. Based on Ecograder's calculations, there are 57 on this page that could slow down page renders. It looks like you stand to optimize a few more of them.
metrics.resources_text: Learn more about how Core Web Vitals improves user experience

metrics.name: Page Interactions
metrics.impact_text: Pages that respond slowly or don't respond as expected to user interactions—like button clicks, taps, key presses, form inputs, and so on—use more energy and frustrate users. This can be attributed to a number of things, some of which are outlined here.
metrics.action_text:
Cumulative Layout Shift100 / 100

Nice work. This page scored 100 for no detectable page layout shifts. Page elements that shift while a user interacts with them are not only annoying but can potentially waste energy too. This is especially true if a user must redo tasks because of shift-induced mistakes they made.

Max Potential First Input Delay78 / 100

This page scored 78 out of 100 based on how quickly users can interact with content. This is pretty good. You can improve this score by identifying page elements that impede speedy interactivity. Based on Ecograder's calculations, page rendering is delayed by 166 millisecond.
metrics.resources_text: Learn how Google's Core Web Vitals and sustainability go hand-in-hand

metrics.name: Optimize Content for Search
metrics.impact_text: Pages that employ SEO best practices help people more quickly find content, reducing energy used while searching. Search-optimized pages also tend to be more accessible and work across a wider array of devices and platforms. This reduces the amount of energy (and frustration) users expend when barriers exist.
metrics.svg:
<svg version="1.1" id="Layer_1" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" x="0px" y="0px"
	 viewBox="0 0 27.12 27.36" enable-background="new 0 0 27.12 27.36" xml:space="preserve">
<circle id="Ellipse_3" fill="none" stroke="#000000" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" cx="10.37" cy="10.37" r="9.37"/>
<line id="Line_11" fill="none" stroke="#000000" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" x1="16.94" y1="17.06" x2="26.12" y2="26.36"/>
</svg>
metrics.action_text:
SEO Score77 / 100

Since search is contextual, it is difficult to estimate precisely how much good SEO practices reduce page-based emissions. Google says a single search can power a lightbulb for 17 seconds. However, given the global volume of daily searches, anything you can do to reduce energy expended by helping people more quickly find content is useful.

metrics.name: Accessibility
metrics.impact_text: Accessible pages tend to work across a wider array of devices and platforms, including assistive technologies used by people with disabilities. Prioritizing accessibility reduces the amount of energy (and frustration) users expend when barriers exist. Plus, it's just the right thing to do.

metrics.action_text:
Accessibility Score96 / 100

An automated crawler like Ecograder can only catch about one-third of total possible accessibility issues on any given page. Plus, emissions estimates related to the ecological impact of accessible content are difficult to quantify. However, truly sustainable solutions are good for people and planet. Because of this, we include web accessibility in Ecograder's scoring algorithm.
metrics.resources_text: Read more about web content, accessibility, and sustainability

metrics.name: Image elements do not have explicit width and height
metrics.impact_text: Set an explicit width and height on image elements to reduce layout shifts and improve CLS.
metrics.action_text:
Unsized images50 / 100

75 images on your web page do not have width and height attributes set. Setting an explicit height and width on image elements helps prevent cumulative layout shift, which can impact user experience and search rankings.

metrics.resources_text: Read why setting width and height on images benefits your users

metrics.name: Avoid multiple page redirects
metrics.impact_text:
Page redirects cause slower page speeds. Pages are flagged if they have two or more redirects.

To prevent this, look at the list of redirects and update links to point directly to each resource’s actual location. This will cut out unnecessary redirecting and reduce load times.
metrics.action_text:
Page redirects100 / 100

Great job. Your page doesn't contain two or more redirects and is optimized for load time based on pointing your resources directly to the actual location.
metrics.resources_text: Read more about how redirects impact page speed

metrics.name: Reduce Server Requests
metrics.impact_text: Every time your page makes a server request, energy is required to transfer and display data. Reducing the number of requests your pages make can improve performance and reduce emissions.
metrics.action_text:
Total Byte Weight0 / 100

This page scored 0 out of 100 possible points for the 10 requests it makes to the server. Try reducing the number of server requests on this page to improve your score.

categories.category_content: Choosing a web host that powers its servers with renewable energy can reduce your digital product or service's environmental impact by an estimated 15%. But not all renewable energy is created equal. If possible, choose a hosting partner that directly powers their data centers with renewables versus one that purchases offsets or unbundled renewable energy credits (RECs).

metrics.name: Green Hosting
metrics.impact_text: Powering servers with renewable energy is an important choice to measurably reduce your product or service's environmental impact.

metrics.action_text:
Green Hosting0 / 100

Hmmm. It appears as though you're not using a web host that powers its servers with renewable energy. You can improve this part of your score by switching to a green web host.
metrics.resources_text:
Find a green web host in The Green Web Foundation's hosting directory

More information on the difference between RECs and renewable energy
